query: Arctic Circle Race cross country ski race Sisimiut

videos.title: Greenland: Arctic Circle Race | Boundless
videos.description:
This three-day cross-country ski race, set north of the Arctic Circle, is long considered one of the most challenging of its kind, due to ever-changing, unpredictable weather conditions.  A harrowing first day out on the mountainous course proves to be full of learning curves, a white-out on day 2 challenges all racers with snow blindness, and a looming storm front on the final day turns the entire race plans upside down.

Subscribe: https://www.youtube.com/outsidetelevision

Find all the best adventure content right here on our YouTube channel and across all of our social media platforms:

Website: http://www.outsidetv.com/
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/outsidetv/
Twitter: https://twitter.com/Outside_TV
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/outsidetv/

For more information on how you can get Outside TV in your home be sure to visit our website: http://www.outsidetv.com/find

And download our FREE app: Outside TV Features - available everywhere! http://watch.outsidetv.com/

Have your own adventure video you want to share with the world? Submit it to Campfire for a chance to win awesome prizes every month! http://campfire.outsidetv.com

videos.title: 2023 Arctic Circle Race - Day 2 Highlights
videos.description:
Highlights from the 2nd day of the 160km/100km Arctic Circle Race - the world's toughest cross-country ski race.  This event is held in Sisimiut, Greenland every year and attracts competitors from around the world.

Find out more:
➡️➡️➡️ Arctic Circle Race:  acr.gl
➡️➡️➡️ Destination Arctic Circle:  destinationarcticcircle.com

Video: Oscar Lange Riis Hansen - Flow Production

❤️ Follow us at @destinationarcticcircle on Facebook and Instagram

videos.title: ARCTIC RACE CIRCLE IN SISIMIUT GREENLAND
videos.description: This video took of my BF.. Please Subscribe my channel.thank you!

videos.title: Arctic Circle Race 2018, Sisimiut, Greenland
videos.description:
A Very Long Video (8 min) of the Arctic Circle Race 2018 in Sisimiut, Greenland.


This is the worlds toughest cross-country adventure race!


Participants from around the world travel 160 km of cross country skiing in the beautiful mountains and scenery of Sisimiut, Greenland.

videos.title: Arctic Circle Race 2019 - Day 3
videos.description:
Drone footage of the start of Day 3 of the Arctic Circle Race 2019 near Sisimiut. The backcountry around Greenland's second largest city is truly spectacular!

Video: Mads Fridolin Vejlby

Music: "Minguitsuugami" by Nanook (awesome Greenlandic Band)
https://www.facebook.com/NanookOfficialFanclub/

videos.title: Arctic Circle Race on Trans World Sport
videos.description: A Trans World Sport feature on the 2010 Arctic Circle Race, a 3 day cross country skiing event in Sisimuit, Greenland.
videos.transcript:
[Music]
straddling the Arctic Circle Greenland
is the world's largest
island covering an area of 2.2 million
square km and with 81% of it ice capped
it's a beautiful inhospitable wilderness
where temperatures in Winter often
plummet to -50°
C an autonomous part of the Kingdom of
Denmark Greenland has a population of
just over 55,000 making it the most
sparsely populated country on
Earth situated on the islands West Coast
imute is the country's second largest
town with a population of just under
6,000 located 65 km inside the Arctic
Circle the town provides the backdrop
for one of the world's toughest annual
CrossCountry skiing events the Arctic
Circle race AR
Circle the Arctic Circle race is the
most important sporting event we have
here takes place every year and attracts
a lot of media coverage which is great
because it allows us to show show off
Greenland from a sporting point of view
it's such an amazing setting that you
can't help but go outside and play some
sort of sport
here
for first held in 1997 the Arctic Circle
race or ACR as it's known is set over 3
days and covers 160
km over the years the race has attracted
some of the world's top CrossCountry ski
including Norway's eight time Olympic
gold medalist bu Daly and this year the
event sees 100 competitors from 14
countries take
part we have many people who come to win
but some just come to challenge
themselves every one of them sees the
Arctic Circle race as an opportunity to
test their own
limits and see if they can finish
it there's no doubt that it has a
positive effect on the competitors daily
lives
afterwards because by the end of the
race both types of participants know
what they are capable of achieving when
they put their mind
to as with greenlandic tradition before
any major sporting event the competitors
attend the local church to receive a
blessing
starting and finishing in simut the race
attracts the whole town as the action
gets underway on a frozen
[Applause]
lake day 1 is a 54.8 km Trek with
difficult and steep gradients to the
campsite which will be for the next two
nights day two is a 50k Loop which
features a climb to the top of k6 a 664
M Peak overlooking sisamot and leads the
competitors back through town before
returning to the camp while day three
follows the same route as day one only
in
reverse the skers are fortunate this
year with the fine weather taking the
sting out of the physical
exertion are you so far this year the
race has been fantastic thanks to the
good
weather it's been a lot of fun and all
the competitors have been really
friendly the course this year is great
too we have lots of up and downhill
sections which makes it
challenging competitors are obliged to
carry safety provisions and his or her
own food supplies the camp has no
washing facilities and the nighttime
temperatures out here can reach -3° C
however as is customary on the second
afternoon of the race the skiers enjoy
greenlandic food prepared by local
hunters and while the event is as much
about competing as it is about enjoying
yourself the organizers are serious
about limiting the impact on the
environment it's crucial to us that
after the Arctic Circle race you can't
spot that the race has taken place it
should look the same as when we
started if any of the as litter they're
disqualified because to do so goes
against the greenlandic way of
thinking helping to monitor this are a
large number of volunteers who follow
the competitors on snowmobiles and dog
sleds they also offer the ski support
Manning checkpoints every 10 km where
they can take on hot drinks and high
energy food
there are about 200 local people who are
working as volunteers all year round to
organize the
ACR during the race we have 150
volunteers helping us out with many
things the event is a big thing for the
community and they really enjoy
supporting the skiers throughout the
race
after 3 days of competition and having
won the first two days Russian skier
Sergei Shirin crosses the Finish Line in
simut to claim the men's title in a time
of 10 hours 8 minutes and 26
seconds it's been great the greenlandic
people here in CIS mute have been really
supportive encouraging us during the
with lots of smiles and positive
emotions for me it's been absolutely
brilliant to race in such a great
atmosphere it's been
unbelievable wins the women's
competition for a second time but the
event is still far from over at the
Arctic Circle race it's not just about
who wins the race but about celebrating
everyone
finishing that
4 hours after spearin finished the
Arctic Circle race officially ends as
Neil skorup and Kim grf cross the
line it's a unique experience for
everyone involved especially the
skiers after three days of competing and
pushing themselves they're exhausted the
crowd is still cheering them on and you
can see how happy the competitors are
when they
finish it's like if they've won it's
quite a touching moment
watch having celebrated its 14th running
this year and attracting competitors
from around the world the Arctic Circle
race looks set to remain Greenland's
number one sporting event for many years
to come
oh

videos.title: Nordenskiöldsloppet 2025. The world’s longest and toughest ski race. The highlights.
videos.description:
This was Nordenskiöldsloppet 2025!

On March 29, 2025, the 8th edition of Nordenskiöldsloppet in modern times took place.
The race follows a historic route from 1884 and leads through untouched wilderness north of the Arctic Circle. It’s an extraordinary skiing adventure spanning 220 km, creating memories that last a lifetime.

Johan Lövgren from Vännäs SK and Sofia Lindberg from Robertsfors IK/Team Edux claimed victory in their respective races with strong performances! 🏆

Register now and write your own hero story.

www.nordenskioldsloppet.com

Video: Vertikal Films Sweden/ Nordenskiöldsloppet 

#Nordenskiöldsloppet #Nordenskiöldsloppet2025 #fischer #skickassics #livetoski #fischernordic #fischerski #xcskiing #xcskiinglife #vasaloppet #skirace #sweden #jokkmokk
